content.subtitle: Use
rows: To say that one person, thing or action differs from another

The city centre is **more crowded than** the suburbs.
Could you speak a little **more slowly**, please?

content.subtitle: Form: adjectives
rows: one syllable ending in vowel + consonant

content.subtitle: Form: adverbs
rows: well → **better**, badly → **worse**, early → **earlier**, near → **nearer**, late → **later**, fast → **faster**, far → **farther/further**

content.subtitle: Useful notes
items: The comparative is usually followed by **than**: *This route is **longer than** the one we took before.*

content.subtitle: Use
rows: To identify the one member of a group that has the most or least of a quality

This is **the most popular** café in the whole city.
Of all the runners, she finished **the fastest**.

content.subtitle: Form: adjectives
rows: one syllable ending in vowel + consonant

content.subtitle: Form: adverbs
rows: well → **best**, badly → **worst**, early → **earliest**, near → **nearest**, late → **latest**, fast → **fastest**, far → **farthest/furthest**

content.subtitle: Useful notes
items: We normally use **the** before a superlative: *That was **the most memorable** concert I've ever been to.*

sections.title: A: Complete with comparative adjectives
sections.instructions: Use the correct comparative form of the adjective in brackets.

items.sentence: This chair is ...... (comfortable) than the old one.
My cousin is ...... (young) than me.
Travelling by train is ...... (slow) than flying.
Today feels ...... (cold) than yesterday.
That documentary was ...... (boring) than I expected.
This restaurant is ...... (good) than the one near the station.
My headache is ...... (bad) today than it was yesterday.
The supermarket is ...... (far) away than the pharmacy.

sections.title: B: Complete with superlative adjectives
sections.instructions: Use the correct superlative form of the adjective in brackets.

items.sentence: This is ...... (quiet) place in the whole building.
Who is ...... (old) person in your class?
That was ...... (amazing) concert I've ever seen.
Friday is ...... (busy) day at the restaurant.
She is ...... (good) student in the course.
This is ...... (bad) hotel I've stayed in.
We found ...... (cheap) option online.
That was ...... (strange) dream I've ever had.

sections.title: C: Comparatives and superlatives of adverbs
sections.instructions: Complete each sentence with the correct form.

items.sentence: Ella dances ...... (gracefully) than her sister.
Out of everyone in the race, Josh ran ...... (fast).
My dad drives ...... (carefully) than my uncle.
Who arrived ...... (late) yesterday?
I played ...... (badly) in the second match.
Of all the speakers, Mia explained the topic ...... (well).
Could you walk a little ...... (slowly)?
He answered the questions ...... (quickly) than the others.

sections.title: D: Choose the correct option
sections.instructions: Circle the correct word or phrase.

items.sentence: This puzzle is **more difficult / difficulter** than the last one.
The Amazon is **the longer / the longest** river in South America.
Your handwriting is **clearer / the clearest** than mine.
Today is **the wetter / the wettest** day this week.
This route is **less expensive / the less expensive** than travelling by plane.
She completed the exercise **more carefully / the most carefully** than anyone else.
Leo is **the funniest / funnier** student in the class.
That was **the least helpful / less helpful** explanation of all.

sections.title: E: Error correction
sections.instructions: Each sentence has one mistake in comparison forms. Find the bold word(s) and write the correct form.

items.sentence: This lesson is **boringer** than yesterday's.
She is the **better** organised student in class.
My suitcase is **biger** than yours.
He studies **most carefully** than his classmates.
This is the **goodest** pizza in town.
Today we arrived **more late** than usual.
Who speaks the **better** in your family?
It's the **most windy** afternoon this month.

sections.title: F: Key word transformation
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence so it means the same.

items.sentence:
This puzzle is easier than the crossword. **DIFFICULT**
The crossword ...... this puzzle.

Nobody in the class speaks as clearly as Emma. **CLEARLY**
Emma ...... in the class.

My old bike wasn't as comfortable as this one. **MORE**
This bike ...... my old one.

Ryan is the strongest player on the team. **THAN**
Ryan is ...... any other player on the team.

This was the least useful app on my phone. **USELESS**
This was ...... app on my phone.

I drive more carefully than my brother. **CAREFULLY**
My brother doesn't drive ...... me.

sections.title: G: Mixed comparison practice
sections.instructions: Each sentence has a mistake in the comparative or superlative form. Write the correct form.

items.sentence: This sofa is **more soft** than the old one.
She is the **most young** teacher at the school.
My new computer is **more fast** than my old one.
He swims **more well** than anyone in the team.
That was the **worstest** meal I've ever eaten.
This coat is **more warm** than that one.
My sister is **more short** than me.
It was the **most foggy** morning of the year.
